title: 7 MTS Administrator Management for Wires
body_text:
7	MTS Administrator Management for Wires
7.1	Overview of MTS Administrators
MTS Administrators use the Money Transfer System (MTS) to manage Wires Users, other MTS Administrators, and branch settings for the Wires application. They also authorize changes in MTS for other MTS Administrators. Only MTS Administrators have access to MTS.
Note:	Access to these functions is based in the MTS Administrator芒聙聶s permissions. MTS Administrators don芒聙聶t have access to the Wires application.
MTS Administrators are set up on two systems. First, the individual must have a Client Centre profile set up as either a Client Centre User or User Management Security Officer. The profile must also have access to the MTS application and a 2-step security token assigned. Complete these steps in the User Management application first, as you will need the individual芒聙聶s Client Centre username to create their MTS Administrator profile in the MTS application. See the User Management Part for procedures.
Second, you must create the User芒聙聶s MTS Administrator芒聙聶s profile in MTS. See Section 7.5, Creating an MTS Administrator, for procedures.
Note: 	Central 1 creates your first two MTS Administrators. You can then add, edit, and delete MTS Administrators and Wires Users as required.
Note:	All Wires User, MTS Administrator, and branch changes require authorization.
7.2	Granting MTS Administrator Access to a Client Centre User or User Management Security Officer
See the User Management Part for procedures on granting MTS Administrator access to Client Centre Users and User Management Security Officers, as well as managing 2-step security tokens.
7.3	Description of Queues in the MTS User Tab
The following queues display on the User tab. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Queues on the User Tab
User Queue
芒聙聯 Select
User
to manage Wires Users.
Admin Queue
芒聙聯 Select
Admin
to manage MTS Administrators.
Change List Queue
芒聙聯 Select
Change List
to authorize or delete pending user and administrator changes.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
7.4	Description of Columns in the MTS Admin Queue
The following table describes the Admin queue columns. See Figure 10. An 芒聙聹X芒聙聺 in a column indicates the user has access to the function.
Column Name
Description
Transit
The MTS Administrator芒聙聶s home transit number.
User ID
The MTS Administrator芒聙聶s user ID (must be the same as the Client Centre User ID).
Name
The MTS Administrator芒聙聶s name.
Admin U
The MTS Administrator can create and update other MTS Administrators.
Admin A
The Administrator can authorize changes to other Administrators芒聙聶 profiles.
Admin T
The MTS Administrator can link branches.
Brn U
The MTS Administrator can update branch settings.
Brn A
The MTS Administrator can authorize changes to branch settings.
User U
The MTS Administrator can create and modify users.
User A
The MTS Administrator can authorize changes to users芒聙聶 profiles.
Figure 10: Admin Queue Columns
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
7.5	Creating an MTS Administrator
The Wires processing model your financial institution selects influences the MTS Administrators芒聙聶 home transit number and permissions. See Chapter 5, Selecting a Wires Processing Model, for a description of centralized, partially centralized, and decentralized Wires environments.
Adding the First Two MTS Administrators
Central 1 adds your financial institution芒聙聶s first two MTS Administrators. These MTS Administrators can create and manage additional MTS Administrators.
To set up the first two MTS Administrators:
Ensure the new MTS Administrators are set up on User Management and assigned access to the MTS application.
Complete the MTS Appointment Certificate, Form 2119, as follows. See Figure 11.
芒聭
Financial Institution Name
芒聢聮
Record the name of your financial institution and the date of the request.
芒聭隆
MTS Administrator 1
芒聢聮
Record the name, contact information, Client Centre User ID (since the MTS User ID must be the same), and the home transit number of the administrator.
芒聢聮
Check the fields beside the permissions to indicate the ones that the Administrator requires. The first two administrators must have all permissions, although the 芒聙聹Linked Transit Assignment芒聙聺 and 芒聙聹Use Linked Transits芒聙聺 permissions aren芒聙聶t mandatory.
芒聭垄
Financial Institution Authorization
芒聢聮
Have two authorized signing officers sign the form.
Figure 11: MTS Appointment Certificate, Form 2119
Forward the form as directed. If applicable, the provincial central will authorize the form and forward it to Central 1. Central 1 will notify you when the administrators are added.
Creating Additional MTS Administrators Using MTS
An MTS Administrator creates additional MTS Administrators as follows:
Ensure the new MTS Administrator is set up on User Management and assigned access to the MTS application.
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS, then select
Admin
. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Access Admin Queue
Select
New
. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: New Administrator
Complete the MTS Administrator芒聙聶s profile as follows. See Figure 14.
芒聭
Enter the MTS User ID. It must be the same as the Client Centre User ID. Don芒聙聶t exceed 20 characters.
芒聭隆
Select the MTS Administrator芒聙聶s home transit number from the drop-down menu.
Note:	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
芒聭垄
Select the check box if the MTS Administrator is authorized to access branches that are linked to their home transit number.
芒聭拢
Select the check box to remove the checkmark and activate the profile.
芒聭陇
Enter the MTS Administrator芒聙聶s name and contact information.
芒聭楼
Select the check box beside the appropriate MTS Administrator permission(s):
芒聢聮
Update Administrator Profile
芒聙聯 Select the check box if the MTS Administrator is authorized to create and modify other administrators.
芒聢聮
Authorize Administrator Update
芒聙聯
Select the check box if the MTS Administrator is permitted to authorize changes to other MTS Administrators芒聙聶 profiles.
芒聢聮
Linked Transit Assignment
芒聙聯
Select the check box if the MTS Administrator is authorized to link branches.
芒聢聮
Update Transit Profile
芒聙聯
Select the check box if the MTS Administrator is authorized to update branch settings.
芒聢聮
Authorize Transit Updates
芒聙聯
Select the check box if the MTS Administrator is permitted to authorize changes to the branch settings.
芒聢聮
Update User Profile
芒聙聯
Select the check box if the MTS Administrator is authorized to create and modify users.
芒聢聮
Authorize User Update
芒聙聯
Select the check box if the MTS Administrator is permitted to authorize changes to users芒聙聶 profiles.
Select
Submit
.
Figure 14: Administrator Set-up
Have another MTS Administrator authorize the new MTS Administrator via the Change List. See Section 7.9, Authorizing Changes to MTS Administrators芒聙聶 Profiles.
Related Information
MTS Appointment Certificate,
Form 2119
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
7.6	Modifying an MTS Administrator
An MTS Administrator modifies another MTS Administrator芒聙聶s permissions and contact information as follows:
Note: 	You can芒聙聶t change the MTS Administrator芒聙聶s user ID and home transit number.
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS, then select
Admin
. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Access Admin Queue
Select a branch from the Transit Number drop-down menu, then select
Go
. See Figure 16.
Figure 16: Selecting a Branch
Note:	Select
All
in the drop-down menu to view MTS Administrators for all branches.
Note:	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
Select the user ID of the MTS Administrator you want to modify. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Selecting MTS Administrator
Tip
:	If there is a pending change to the MTS Administrator芒聙聶s profile, it won芒聙聶t appear in the Admin queue. Authorize or cancel the pending change before proceeding.
Modify the MTS Administrator as required. See Section 7.5, Creating an MTS Administrator, for a description of each field.
Select
Update
. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: Modify Administrator
Have another MTS Administrator authorize the change via the Change List. See Section 7.9, Authorizing Changes to MTS Administrators芒聙聶 Profiles.
Tip
:	An MTS Administrator can芒聙聶t change their MTS profile but can authorize changes to their profile.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
7.7	Deleting an MTS Administrator
An MTS Administrator deletes an MTS Administrator as follows:
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS, then select
Admin
. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: Access Admin Queue
Select a branch from the Transit Number drop-down menu and select
Go
. See Figure 20.
Note:	Select
All
in the drop-down menu to view MTS Administrators for all branches.
Note:	Multiple branches display in the drop-down menu if other transit numbers are linked to your home transit number, and you have permission to access the linked branches.
Figure 20: Selecting a Branch
Select the user ID of the MTS Administrator you want to delete. See Figure 21.
Figure 21: Selecting an MTS Administrator
Tip
:	If there are pending changes to the MTS Administrator芒聙聶s profile, they won芒聙聶t appear in the Admin queue. Cancel the pending changes before proceeding.
Select
Delete
. See Figure 22.
Figure 22: Delete Administrator
Have another MTS Administrator authorize the change. See Section 7.9, Authorizing Changes to MTS Administrators芒聙聶 Profiles.
Have a User Management Security Officer remove the MTS application from the MTS Administrator芒聙聶s Client Centre user profile or delete their profile if Client Centre Access is no longer required (e.g., no longer your employee). See the User Management Part for procedures.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
7.8	Description of Columns in the MTS Change List Queue (Users and Administrators)
The Change List on the User tab provides a list of unauthorized MTS User and MTS Administrator changes. The following table describes the Change List queue columns. See Figure 23.
Column Name
Description
Transit
The home transit number of the user or administrator.
User ID
The MTS user ID of the individual. Must be the same as the Client Centre User ID.
Type
The profile type, either User (Wires User) or an Administrator (MTS Administrator).
Name
The individual芒聙聶s name
Daily Limit
The maximum value of outgoing wires that can be approved in a business day.
Transfer Limit
The maximum value of an outgoing wire that can be approved.
Committed
The total amount of outgoing wires that the user approved during the day. Resets to zero at the end of the business day.
Balance
The daily limit subtracted from the committed amount.
Action
The type of change (e.g., New, Update, or Delete).
Figure 23: Change List
Note:	Limits apply to Wires Users assigned the 芒聙聹Authorize Outgoing Transfers芒聙聺 permission. Limits are in Canadian dollars. Wires created in other currencies are compared to the user芒聙聶s limits but at the Canadian equivalent amount.
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
7.9	Authorizing Changes to MTS Administrators芒聙聶 Profiles
When you add, modify, or delete an MTS Administrator , a second MTS Administrator must authorize the change.
Tip
:
An MTS Administrator can芒聙聶t change their MTS profile but can authorize changes to their profile.
To Authorize changes to an MTS Administrator:
Log in to MTS as described in Section 6.2, Logging in to MTS, then select
Change List
. See Figure 24.
Tip
:	The number next to 芒聙聹User Changes芒聙聺 indicates the number of unauthorized Wires User and MTS Administrator changes.
Figure 24: Access Change List
Select the user ID of the MTS Administrator you want to authorize. See Figure 25.
Figure 25: Select User ID
Review the change, then select
Authorize
. See Figure 26. Alternatively, select
Cancel
to delete the change.
Figure 26: Authorize Change
Related Information
Training Videos:
Using MTS for Wires User and Branch Settings
END
